patch.value: Promote history-stream maintenance primitives to the public SDK: history-redact, history-repair, history-compact (audited rewrite, re-anchor, checkpoint/prune)

The existing promotion set (pm-98cz lifecycle, pm-rjqr reads, pm-zwpp facets, pm-oxrw governance, pm-3mna workspace config) leaves the history MUTATION family uncovered: history-redact (audited redaction), history-repair (chain re-anchor after drift), history-compact (checkpoint/prune preserving replay integrity), all built on the shared history-rewrite orchestration (dedup'd in closed pm-bzgt). SDK-first requires these as public typed primitives so the consolidated pm history subcommands (pm-tqel) become thin shims and integrators can run stream maintenance programmatically. Duplicate check: pm-tqel is the CLI-grammar counterpart; pm-xk39/pm-bzgt closed history; no open SDK-promotion overlap.

SDK exports typed redact/repair/compact primitives preserving audited-rewrite guarantees (hash chain continuity, replay integrity, checkpoint markers, redaction audit trail) and returning the same structured results the CLI prints; CLI history maintenance commands consume only these primitives; docstrings + linked tests + SDK reference docs; zero behavior change (history rewrite/compact/redact specs green).
